metadata.title: MCP Official Security Foundations → AppSec Core V1 (with ACR-001, ACR-002, ACR-004 incorporated) (Concept Crosswalk)
metadata.schema-basis: NIST IR 8477 relationship vocabulary + NIST IR 8278A r1 STRM resource model

reference-document.title: AppSec Core V1 (with ACR-001, ACR-002, ACR-004 incorporated)
reference-document.author: SbD-ToE Programme — AppSec Core ontology project

metadata.methodology: Threshold-based continuous→categorical translation: sim≥0.6 → equal; 0.4≤sim<0.6 → intersects-with; sim<0.4 → unspecified. Multi-claim per source-item: one row per (source-item, target-Core-entity) unique pair using highest-similarity claim for that target. is-primary="true" marks item overall highest-similarity claim's target. subset-of / superset-of NOT in first cut (cosine similarity lacks directional info; SME refinement future work). STRM resource model: pilot doc → DocumentaryImpl; source-item → ConcreteImpl; AppSec Core V1 → Reference Document; Core entity → Concept.

source-element.description: Authorization server discovery and protected resource metadata. This section describes the mechanisms by which MCP servers advertise their associated authorization servers to MCP clients, as well as …

reference-element.description: Authorization Policy Integrity And Least Privilege. Maintain explicit authorization policy and identity-to-permission binding under least-privilege constraints, including role-based and attribute-bas…

mapping.rationale: Cosine similarity 0.474; threshold mapping → intersects-with. Claim MCP_OFFICIAL_SECURITY_FOUNDATIONS_2025-CLAIM-000001 at ControlObjective level. Primary anchor (item highest-similarity claim).

source-element.description: Authorization server discovery and protected resource metadata. This section describes the mechanisms by which MCP servers advertise their associated authorization servers to MCP clients, as well as …

reference-element.description: Registry Allowlisting And Approved Source Enforcement. Limit acquisition and promotion to approved repositories, registries and trusted base-image sources. Technical controls that restrict dependency…

mapping.rationale: Cosine similarity 0.415; threshold mapping → intersects-with. Claim MCP_OFFICIAL_SECURITY_FOUNDATIONS_2025-CLAIM-000002 at Mechanism level. Secondary disclosure.

source-element.description: Scope selection and step-up authorization. When implementing authorization flows, MCP clients SHOULD follow the principle of least privilege by requesting only the scopes necessary for their intended…

reference-element.description: Authorization Policy Integrity And Least Privilege. Maintain explicit authorization policy and identity-to-permission binding under least-privilege constraints, including role-based and attribute-bas…

mapping.rationale: Cosine similarity 0.445; threshold mapping → intersects-with. Claim MCP_OFFICIAL_SECURITY_FOUNDATIONS_2025-CLAIM-000003 at ControlObjective level. Primary anchor (item highest-similarity claim).

source-element.description: Inbound token handling and validation. Access token handling when making requests to MCP servers MUST conform to the requirements defined in OAuth 2.1 Section 5 “Resource Requests” . Specifically: MC…

reference-element.description: API Gateway With Mutual Authentication. Review trust boundaries and integration paths explicitly before accepting exposure or interface changes. Bind service interactions to explicit trusted identiti…

mapping.rationale: Cosine similarity 0.411; threshold mapping → intersects-with. Claim MCP_OFFICIAL_SECURITY_FOUNDATIONS_2025-CLAIM-000004 at Mechanism level. Primary anchor (item highest-similarity claim).

source-element.description: Authorization error handling and insufficient-scope behavior. Servers MUST return appropriate HTTP status codes for authorization errors: Status Code Description Usage 401 Unauthorized Authorization …

reference-element.description: Controlled Failure And Non-Revealing Client Error Surface. Validation and processing failures must fail closed with non-revealing client responses that avoid stack traces, resource enumeration and in…

reference-element.description: Authenticated API Boundary Enforcement. Authenticate callers and enforce authorization decisions consistently at API and service boundaries. identity and trust control. gateway enforcement

mapping.rationale: Cosine similarity 0.462; threshold mapping → intersects-with. Claim MCP_OFFICIAL_SECURITY_FOUNDATIONS_2025-CLAIM-000006 at Practice level. Secondary disclosure.

reference-element.description: Access Policy Enforcement. Keep permission models explicit, separated by role or attribute and bounded by least-privilege rules. Authenticate callers and enforce authorization decisions consistently …

source-element.description: Token passthrough anti-pattern. “Token passthrough” is an anti-pattern where an MCP server accepts tokens from an MCP client without validating that the tokens were properly issued to the MCP server …

reference-element.description: Session And Token Trust Boundaries. Sessions and tokens must be unguessable, transported securely, bounded in scope and lifetime, and revalidated or revoked when client context materially changes. Se…

mapping.rationale: Cosine similarity 0.485; threshold mapping → intersects-with. Claim MCP_OFFICIAL_SECURITY_FOUNDATIONS_2025-CLAIM-000014 at ControlObjective level. Secondary disclosure.

source-element.description: Session hijacking and event injection. Session hijacking is an attack vector where a client is provided a session ID by the server, and an unauthorized party is able to obtain and use that same sessi…

reference-element.description: Bounded Session And Token Management. Keep session identifiers and tokens bounded by transport, scope, TTL and explicit invalidation rules. identity and trust control. session management

reference-element.description: Centralized Log Ingestion And Source Accountability. Security logs must be ingested centrally with source accountability so that review does not depend on fragile local host access or ad hoc collecti…
